questionHtml: With reference to the Lagrange form of the error term, explain whether your answer to part (b) is an overestimate or an underestimate for f(0.4).
markschemeHtml:
f7c<0  (for all c ∈ ]0, 0.4[)       R1


Note: Accept R6c<0 or “the error term is negative”.


the answer in (b) is an overestimate       A1


Note: The A1 is dependent on the R1.


[2 marks]

meta.title: With reference to the Lagrange form of the error term, explain whether your answer to part (b) is an overestimate or an underestimate for f(0.4).
